sections.title: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles

items.title: Wildcard actions and resources in IAM policies
items.description: Policies containing "Action": "*" or "Resource": "*" (AWS), Owner/Contributor at subscription scope (Azure), or roles/owner and primitive roles (GCP) grant excessive access that violates least privilege.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Confirm whether any human or service identity is bound to them
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for wildcard actions and resources in iam policies, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Known IAM privilege-escalation paths
items.description: Permissions such as iam:CreatePolicyVersion, iam:PassRole + ec2:RunInstances, lambda:UpdateFunctionCode, or sts:AssumeRole chains can be abused to escalate from a low-privileged identity to admin.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Attempt safe enumeration in an authorized test account
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for known iam privilege-escalation paths, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: iam:PassRole and actAs abuse
items.description: An identity allowed to pass a more privileged role to a compute service (EC2, Lambda, ECS, GCE, Cloud Functions) can assume that role indirectly and inherit its permissions.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Identity & Access Management — Privilege Escalation & Over-Privileged Roles: Find identities with PassRole/actAs combined with a service that runs the role
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for iam:passrole and actas abuse, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Unused and stale permissions
items.description: Permissions and identities granted but never used widen the attack surface.
items.how_to_find: Compare granted permissions against last-used data (AWS Access Advisor, GCP IAM Recommender). Report permissions unused beyond the policy window as candidates for removal.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for unused and stale permissions, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials

items.title: Leaked access keys in code, repos, and artifacts
items.description: AWS access keys, Azure client secrets, and GCP service account JSON keys are frequently committed to source control, container images, CI logs, and public buckets.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Validate any discovered key against the cloud API (e.g., aws sts get-caller-identity) to confirm it is live
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for leaked access keys in code, repos, and artifacts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Long-lived static credentials
items.description: Static keys without expiry (AWS IAM user keys, Azure SP secrets, GCP SA keys) should be replaced by short-lived roles, workload identity federation, or instance/managed identities.
items.how_to_find: Inventory all static credentials and their age. Flag any long-lived key where a federated or role-based alternative is available.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for long-lived static credentials, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: GCP service account key best practices

items.title: Missing or stale key rotation
items.description: Credentials not rotated within policy windows increase the impact of a leak.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Identity & Access Management — Access Keys & Long-Lived Credentials: Check create/last-rotated timestamps for all keys and secrets
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for missing or stale key rotation, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Active credentials on inactive or dormant identities
items.description: Service accounts and users that are no longer used but still hold active keys are prime targets for credential-stuffing and reuse.
items.how_to_find: Correlate credential last-used data with identity activity. Flag active keys belonging to identities with no recent API activity.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for active credentials on inactive or dormant identities, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK - Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts

sections.title: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene

items.title: Missing MFA on console/privileged users
items.description: Human identities with console access and no MFA are vulnerable to phishing and password reuse.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Enumerate users with passwords/interactive sign-in and check MFA enrollment
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for missing mfa on console/privileged users, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Root / Global Admin account usage
items.description: The AWS root account, Azure Global Administrator, and GCP super admin should be locked down with MFA, no access keys, and used only for break-glass scenarios.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Check for recent root/super-admin sign-ins and API activity, presence of root access keys, and MFA status
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for root / global admin account usage, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Weak MFA factors on highly privileged identities
items.description: SMS and TOTP can be phished; phishing-resistant factors (FIDO2/hardware keys) are recommended for break-glass and admin identities.
items.how_to_find: Review configured MFA factor types for privileged identities. Flag privileged accounts relying solely on phishable factors.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for weak mfa factors on highly privileged identities, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Access keys present on root/super-admin
items.description: Root and super-admin accounts should never have programmatic access keys; their existence drastically increases blast radius if leaked.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Identity & Access Management — MFA & Root/Privileged Account Hygiene: Confirm no access keys exist for the AWS root account or GCP organization super admin
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for access keys present on root/super-admin, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Identity & Access Management — Trust Policies & Federation

items.title: Over-broad assume-role trust policies
items.description: Trust policies allowing Principal "*" or entire external accounts to assume a role can let attackers pivot into the account.
items.how_to_find: Enumerate role trust policies and flag wildcard principals, broad external account trust, or trust without condition keys.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-broad assume-role trust policies, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Confused deputy via missing ExternalId / condition
items.description: Cross-account roles assumed by third parties must enforce sts:ExternalId or source conditions to prevent a confused-deputy attack.
items.how_to_find: Identity & Access Management — Trust Policies & Federation: Identity & Access Management — Trust Policies & Federation: Identity & Access Management — Trust Policies & Federation: Identity & Access Management — Trust Policies & Federation: Identity & Access Management — Trust Policies & Federation: Check third-party-assumable roles for ExternalId or aws:SourceAccount/SourceArn conditions
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for confused deputy via missing externalid / condition, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Misconfigured OIDC / workload identity federation
items.description: GitHub Actions OIDC, GCP Workload Identity Federation, and Azure federated credentials with overly broad subject/audience claims let untrusted repos or workloads assume cloud roles.
items.how_to_find: Inspect OIDC provider trust conditions (sub, aud, repository claims). Flag wildcards or missing claim restrictions that allow any repo/branch to assume the role.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for misconfigured oidc / workload identity federation, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Excessive service principal / managed identity grants
items.description: Azure service principals and managed identities, and GCP service accounts, with broad directory or project roles can be abused for lateral movement and persistence.
items.how_to_find: Enumerate service principal/SA role assignments and API permissions (e.g., Graph application permissions). Flag high-privilege grants such as directory-wide write or project owner.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for excessive service principal / managed identity grants, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Public-read buckets / containers
items.description: S3 buckets, Azure blob containers, and GCS buckets granting read to AllUsers/anonymous expose data to anyone on the internet.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Attempt unauthenticated reads to confirm exposure
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for public-read buckets / containers, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Public-write / world-writable buckets
items.description: Buckets allowing anonymous PUT/DELETE enable defacement, malware hosting, and supply-chain attacks on assets served from the bucket.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Test for anonymous write permission against discovered buckets in authorized scope
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for public-write / world-writable buckets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Overly permissive bucket resource policies
items.description: Resource policies with wildcard principals or broad cross-account access can expose data even when ACLs appear restricted.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Confirm whether Block Public Access / public access prevention overrides them
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for overly permissive bucket resource policies, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Dangling / claimable bucket names
items.description: References to deleted buckets (in code, DNS, or web assets) can be re-registered by attackers to serve malicious content (bucket takeover/squatting).
items.how_to_find: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Storage — Public Buckets & ACL/Policy Misconfiguration: Identify bucket references that return NoSuchBucket and confirm the name is registerable in the same provider/region
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for dangling / claimable bucket names, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: Hacking the Cloud - S3 bucket takeover

sections.title: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management

items.title: Unencrypted storage at rest
items.description: Buckets, disks, and databases without encryption at rest fail most compliance baselines and increase impact of physical or backup compromise.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Enumerate storage resources and confirm default encryption is enabled
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for unencrypted storage at rest, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Weak KMS key policies and shared keys
items.description: KMS/Key Vault key policies with wildcard principals, or use of a single shared key across sensitive datasets, weaken cryptographic isolation.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Review key policies for over-broad principals and check whether sensitive data uses dedicated customer-managed keys
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for weak kms key policies and shared keys, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Missing key rotation
items.description: KMS/Key Vault keys without automatic rotation increase exposure window if a key is compromised.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Check rotation settings for customer-managed keys
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for missing key rotation, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Encryption in transit not enforced
items.description: Storage that allows plaintext HTTP access (no aws:SecureTransport / HTTPS-only) risks interception of data in transit.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Storage — Encryption at Rest & Key Management: Check bucket policies / settings for enforced TLS (deny non-HTTPS)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for encryption in transit not enforced, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Pre-signed / SAS URL abuse
items.description: Long-lived or over-scoped pre-signed URLs (S3), SAS tokens (Azure), and signed URLs (GCS) can grant broad, hard-to-revoke access if leaked.
items.how_to_find: Review generation patterns for signed URLs/SAS tokens: expiry duration, scope (object vs container), and permissions. Flag long-lived or account-level SAS tokens.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for pre-signed / sas url abuse, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Object listing enabled
items.description: Buckets that allow listing let attackers enumerate all object keys, turning a single leak into full data discovery.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Test whether ListBucket/list is permitted for the relevant principal (including anonymous)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for object listing enabled, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Sensitive data in object versions / backups
items.description: Deleted or rotated secrets may persist in object version history, snapshots, and lifecycle-managed backups accessible to over-privileged principals.
items.how_to_find: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Storage — Signed URLs & Object Listing: Check whether versioning exposes prior object versions to readers and whether old versions contain secrets
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for sensitive data in object versions / backups, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: IMDSv1 enabled (no token requirement)
items.description: IMDSv1 responds to simple GET requests, so any SSRF on the instance can read metadata and steal role credentials.
items.how_to_find: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Check instance metadata options; flag instances allowing IMDSv1 (HttpTokens=optional)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for imdsv1 enabled (no token requirement), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Role credential theft via metadata SSRF
items.description: Reaching /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ (AWS), the Azure IMDS token endpoint, or the GCP metadata endpoint exposes temporary credentials usable from anywhere.
items.how_to_find: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Where an authorized SSRF exists, attempt to retrieve role/identity credentials from the metadata endpoint and confirm they authenticate via the provider API
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for role credential theft via metadata ssrf, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Azure/GCP metadata header bypass exposure
items.description: Azure IMDS requires the Metadata:true header and GCP requires Metadata-Flavor:Google; SSRF that can set arbitrary headers can still reach them.
items.how_to_find: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Test whether reachable apps allow attacker-controlled headers to the metadata endpoint
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for azure/gcp metadata header bypass exposure, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: IMDSv2 hop limit allows container access
items.description: A metadata hop limit greater than 1 lets containers on the host reach IMDS, expanding the SSRF/credential-theft surface to compromised containers.
items.how_to_find: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Compute & Metadata — Instance Metadata & SSRF: Check instance metadata hop limit; flag values above 1 on hosts running untrusted or internet-facing containers
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for imdsv2 hop limit allows container access, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Compute & Metadata — Instance Profiles & User-Data Secrets

items.title: Over-privileged instance profiles / identities
items.description: Instance profiles (AWS), managed identities (Azure), and attached service accounts (GCP) with broad permissions become a powerful pivot if the host is compromised.
items.how_to_find: Enumerate roles/identities attached to compute and assess effective permissions against least privilege. Flag admin-equivalent or broad data access.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-privileged instance profiles / identities, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Secrets in user-data / startup scripts
items.description: Passwords, API keys, and tokens placed in EC2 user-data, Azure custom data, or GCP startup-script metadata are readable by any process that can reach the metadata service.
items.how_to_find: Retrieve user-data/startup-script metadata and scan for embedded secrets. Any plaintext credential is a finding.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for secrets in user-data / startup scripts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Secrets in instance/container environment
items.description: Credentials injected as environment variables on instances and containers can leak via debug endpoints, process listing, or compromised dependencies.
items.how_to_find: Review how secrets reach compute (env vs secret manager). Flag plaintext secrets in environment or task/pod definitions.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for secrets in instance/container environment, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Remote-access / agent paths to instances
items.description: SSM Session Manager (AWS), Azure Bastion/Run Command, and GCP OS Login/serial console can grant interactive access; over-broad permission to these is a stealthy compromise path.
items.how_to_find: Compute & Metadata — Instance Profiles & User-Data Secrets: Compute & Metadata — Instance Profiles & User-Data Secrets: Compute & Metadata — Instance Profiles & User-Data Secrets: Compute & Metadata — Instance Profiles & User-Data Secrets: Compute & Metadata — Instance Profiles & User-Data Secrets: Check which identities can run commands or start sessions on instances (ssm:StartSession, RunCommand, OS Login)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for remote-access / agent paths to instances, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks

items.title: Public AMIs / shared images
items.description: AMIs (AWS), images (GCP), and VM images (Azure) shared publicly or with external accounts may contain baked-in secrets, source code, or configuration.
items.how_to_find: Enumerate images and their sharing settings. Flag any image shared publicly or with unexpected accounts and inspect for embedded secrets.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for public amis / shared images, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Public EBS / disk snapshots
items.description: Publicly shared EBS snapshots, Azure managed disk snapshots, and GCP disk snapshots can be mounted by attackers to extract data and credentials.
items.how_to_find: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Where authorized, mount a copy to confirm sensitive content
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for public ebs / disk snapshots, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Snapshots shared with untrusted accounts
items.description: Snapshots and images shared with specific external accounts can be exfiltrated if those accounts are compromised or no longer trusted.
items.how_to_find: Review explicit account share lists on images/snapshots and validate each external account is current and authorized.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for snapshots shared with untrusted accounts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Unencrypted snapshots and volumes
items.description: Unencrypted volumes and snapshots increase impact when shared or copied, since data is readable without keys.
items.how_to_find: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Compute & Metadata — Public Images, Snapshots & Disks: Check encryption status of volumes and snapshots
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for unencrypted snapshots and volumes, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Management ports open to 0.0.0.0/0
items.description: SSH (22), RDP (3389), and admin ports exposed to the entire internet via security groups, NSGs, or VPC firewall rules invite brute-force and exploitation.
items.how_to_find: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Confirm reachability of exposed hosts
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for management ports open to 0.0.0.0/0, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Databases / data services exposed publicly
items.description: Database ports (3306, 5432, 1433, 27017, 6379) and managed data services reachable from the internet are high-value targets and frequent breach causes.
items.how_to_find: Detect public ingress to database/data ports and managed service public endpoints. Flag any internet-reachable data service.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for databases / data services exposed publicly, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Overly broad allow rules
items.description: Rules permitting all ports/protocols from wide ranges undermine segmentation and make lateral movement easy after a single compromise.
items.how_to_find: Review rules for all-port/all-protocol grants and large source ranges. Flag rules far broader than the service requires.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for overly broad allow rules, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Use of default / permissive network groups
items.description: Default security groups and unrestricted default firewall rules often carry permissive settings and should not be used for production workloads.
items.how_to_find: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Check whether resources rely on default security groups/firewalls and whether those defaults are locked down
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for use of default / permissive network groups, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Over-broad VPC peering / VNet connections
items.description: Wide peering or hub-spoke designs without route filtering can let a compromise in one network reach many others.
items.how_to_find: Map peering/VNet connections and route tables. Flag peerings that grant broader reachability than necessary.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-broad vpc peering / vnet connections, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Missing private endpoints for managed services
items.description: Accessing managed services over public endpoints instead of PrivateLink/Private Endpoint/Private Service Connect exposes traffic and broadens attack surface.
items.how_to_find: Identify managed-service access paths and flag services reachable only via public endpoints where private connectivity is available.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for missing private endpoints for managed services, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Unrestricted egress / data exfiltration paths
items.description: Unrestricted outbound traffic enables data exfiltration and C2; egress filtering and VPC service controls limit this.
items.how_to_find: Review egress rules and exfiltration controls (e.g., GCP VPC Service Controls, egress firewall). Flag fully open egress from sensitive workloads.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for unrestricted egress / data exfiltration paths, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Flat networks with no tiering
items.description: Workloads of differing trust levels in a single flat subnet allow unrestricted lateral movement after initial access.
items.how_to_find: Review subnet/tier design and intra-network rules. Flag flat designs lacking separation between public, app, and data tiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for flat networks with no tiering, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Dangling CNAME to deprovisioned cloud resource
items.description: CNAMEs pointing to released cloud endpoints (S3 website, CloudFront, Azure App Service, Storage, GCP buckets) can be re-registered by attackers to take over the subdomain.
items.how_to_find: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Confirm the target endpoint name is claimable
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for dangling cname to deprovisioned cloud resource, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Takeover of storage/website endpoints
items.description: DNS aliases to S3/GCS static website endpoints or Azure Storage that have been deleted let an attacker create a same-named resource and serve content under the victim domain.
items.how_to_find: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Identify aliases to storage/website endpoints returning not-found, and verify the underlying bucket/account name can be re-created
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for takeover of storage/website endpoints, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Dangling A record to released elastic/public IP
items.description: A records pointing to released public/elastic IPs can resolve to an attacker who later acquires that IP from the provider pool.
items.how_to_find: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Check A records against currently allocated IPs in the account
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for dangling a record to released elastic/public ip, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Public DNS zone information disclosure
items.description: Overly detailed public DNS records (internal hostnames, service identifiers) aid reconnaissance and target selection.
items.how_to_find: Review public zone records for internal naming and sensitive service hints. Flag records that leak architecture details.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for public dns zone information disclosure, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection

items.title: Over-privileged function execution roles
items.description: Functions running with broad execution roles can be abused for privilege escalation or data access if the function is compromised via injection or a vulnerable dependency.
items.how_to_find: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Functions running with broad execution roles can be abused for privilege escalation or data access if the function is compromised via injection or a vulnerable dependency
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-privileged function execution roles, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Secrets in function environment variables
items.description: API keys and credentials stored as function environment variables can be read by anyone with read access to the function configuration.
items.how_to_find: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Inspect function environment variables for plaintext secrets and check who can read function config
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for secrets in function environment variables, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Event-data injection into functions
items.description: Functions triggered by untrusted events (HTTP, queue, object, email) that pass input to commands, queries, or downstream APIs are vulnerable to injection.
items.how_to_find: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Test for injection where authorized
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for event-data injection into functions, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Publicly invokable functions / function URLs
items.description: Function URLs, public API Gateway routes, or anonymous HTTP triggers without auth let anyone invoke business logic directly.
items.how_to_find: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Serverless & PaaS — Functions: Privilege, Secrets & Injection: Identify function URLs and HTTP triggers and check auth configuration
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for publicly invokable functions / function urls, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Over-broad function resource policies
items.description: Resource-based policies allowing wildcard principals or arbitrary services to invoke a function widen the trigger surface and may enable cross-account invocation.
items.how_to_find: Review function resource policies for wildcard principals or unexpected service/account grants. Flag over-broad invoke permissions.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-broad function resource policies, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Secrets in code and configuration instead of a vault
items.description: Hardcoded secrets in repos, IaC, and config bypass vault access controls, rotation, and audit logging.
items.how_to_find: Scan source, IaC, and config for secrets and compare against vault usage. Flag secrets that should be in Secrets Manager / Key Vault / Secret Manager.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for secrets in code and configuration instead of a vault, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Over-broad access to secret stores
items.description: Wide read access to Secrets Manager, Key Vault, or Secret Manager lets many identities retrieve sensitive material, expanding leak impact.
items.how_to_find: Review who can read each secret/vault. Flag broad or wildcard read access and missing per-secret scoping.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-broad access to secret stores, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Secrets without rotation or expiry
items.description: Long-lived secrets in the vault that are never rotated remain valid indefinitely if leaked.
items.how_to_find: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Check rotation configuration and age for stored secrets
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for secrets without rotation or expiry, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: No auditing on secret access
items.description: Without logging of secret retrieval, abnormal access (e.g., mass reads after a compromise) goes undetected.
items.how_to_find: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Serverless & PaaS — Secrets Management: Confirm secret-access events are logged (CloudTrail data events, Key Vault diagnostics, audit logs) and retained
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for no auditing on secret access, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance

items.title: Audit logging disabled or incomplete
items.description: Missing CloudTrail trails, disabled Azure Activity/diagnostic logs, or absent GCP Data Access logs leave actions unrecorded, crippling detection and forensics.
items.how_to_find: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Confirm an organization-wide, multi-region trail/log sink is enabled and covers management and key data events
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for audit logging disabled or incomplete, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Log tampering / deletion possible
items.description: Trails and log buckets without integrity validation, immutability, or restricted delete permissions can be tampered with to hide attacker activity.
items.how_to_find: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Check log file validation, bucket object lock/immutability, and who can stop/delete trails or logs
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for log tampering / deletion possible, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: AWS CloudTrail log file integrity validation

items.title: Data-plane / object access events not logged
items.description: Management-only logging misses S3/object data events, Key Vault secret reads, and storage access, hiding data exfiltration.
items.how_to_find: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Logging & Monitoring — Audit Logging & Tamper Resistance: Confirm data events are logged for sensitive resources (S3 data events, Storage diagnostics, GCP Data Access logs)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for data-plane / object access events not logged, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Insufficient log retention
items.description: Short retention windows prevent investigation of incidents discovered late and may breach compliance requirements.
items.how_to_find: Review retention settings for audit logs and log sinks. Flag retention below policy/compliance requirements.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for insufficient log retention, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Threat detection service disabled
items.description: GuardDuty, Defender for Cloud, or Security Command Center not enabled across all accounts/regions leaves cloud-native threats undetected.
items.how_to_find: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Confirm detection services are enabled organization-wide and across regions
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for threat detection service disabled, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: No alerting / notification on findings
items.description: Detection without alerting (no SNS/EventBridge, Action Group, or Pub/Sub notification) means findings sit unreviewed.
items.how_to_find: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Verify findings route to an alerting pipeline and on-call
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for no alerting / notification on findings, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detection feature/coverage gaps
items.description: Detection enabled but with key protections off (e.g., GuardDuty S3/EKS/malware protection, Defender plans per resource type) leaves blind spots.
items.how_to_find: Review which detection plans/features are enabled per resource type. Flag disabled high-value protections.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detection feature/coverage gaps, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: No posture management / config baseline
items.description: Without continuous posture management (Security Hub, Defender CSPM, SCC posture), drift from secure baselines goes unnoticed.
items.how_to_find: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Logging & Monitoring — Threat Detection Coverage: Confirm a CSPM/posture service evaluates resources against a benchmark continuously
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for no posture management / config baseline, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.
